questions.question: What is Responsive Design & Media Queries and why is it important?
questions.answer: Responsive Design & Media Queries is a fundamental concept that Build pages that work on every screen size with breakpoints. It's important because it forms the foundation for building robust and efficient applications. Understanding Responsive Design & Media Queries allows developers to make better architectural decisions and write cleaner code.

questions.question: How do you apply Responsive Design & Media Queries in a real project?
questions.answer: In practice, Responsive Design & Media Queries is applied by following established patterns and best practices. Start with the simplest implementation, test thoroughly, and iterate. The key is to understand the underlying principles so you can adapt to different scenarios.

questions.question: What are common mistakes when working with Responsive Design & Media Queries?
questions.answer: Common mistakes include: not understanding the fundamentals before applying advanced patterns, ignoring edge cases, not testing adequately, and over-engineering solutions. The best approach is to start simple and add complexity only when needed.

questions.question: How would you explain Responsive Design & Media Queries to a beginner?
questions.answer: Responsive Design & Media Queries is essentially about build pages that work on every screen size with breakpoints. Think of it as a building block — once you understand this concept, you can combine it with other concepts to build complex applications.
